news.headline: Nasdaq +1.12%, S&P 500 +0.72% on Thursday before July 4 US holiday; Dow crosses 53,000
news.what_happened: US equities posted strong gains on Thursday July 3 ahead of the Independence Day holiday. S&P 500 closed at 7,537 (+0.72%), Nasdaq at 26,121 (+1.12%), Dow first close above 53,000.
news.why_it_matters: Nasdaq +1.12% is the strongest single catalyst for Indian IT stocks. TCS, Infosys, HCLTECH derive 50-60% revenues from North American clients. FIIs are likely to buy large-cap IT on Monday open.

news.headline: OPEC+ approves 188,000 bpd production hike for August — fifth consecutive monthly increase
news.what_happened: Seven OPEC+ nations (Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria, Oman) agreed to raise output by 188,000 bpd from August 2026, continuing a progressive unwinding of 2023 production cuts. WTI crude is at $69.23 (+0.99%) despite the supply news.
news.why_it_matters: Crude remains elevated (+0.99%) today, pressuring airlines, paints, tyres. However, medium-term supply ramp signals eventual crude price decline. OMCs (HPCL, BPCL) see short-term inventory gains; upstream ONGC sees higher realization today.

news.headline: Samsung falls 6.9% on chip oversupply fears; Nikkei -1.19%, Kospi -5.3%
news.what_happened: Samsung Electronics dropped 6.9% despite reporting a 19x surge in quarterly profit, as investors fear semiconductor oversupply. Dragged Nikkei 225 down 1.19% and Kospi down 5.3%.
news.why_it_matters: Hardware chip sector sell-off in Asia. Indian IT is software services — limited direct exposure. But global tech sentiment caution may slightly cap IT upside today. Watch for any follow-through selling.

news.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Rules of origin notified; effective July 15, 2026
news.what_happened: India's finance ministry notified detailed rules of origin under the India-UK FTA, clearing the way for implementation from July 15, 2026. Indian exporters get preferential tariff access to UK markets.
news.why_it_matters: Textile, pharma, engineering goods exporters benefit from preferential access. Sentiment boost for export-oriented sectors ahead of actual implementation. UK is India's 5th-largest trading partner.

news.headline: RBI keeps repo rate at 5.25% with neutral stance; GDP growth pegged at 6.9% for FY27
news.what_happened: RBI MPC unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral policy stance. GDP growth for FY2026-27 projected at 6.9%, CPI inflation at 4.6%. RBI also issued advisory on cybersecurity threats from Mythos AI.
news.why_it_matters: No fresh rate catalyst for banking or real estate sectors today. Status quo is broadly neutral. GDP growth of 6.9% supports earnings momentum for banks and consumer-facing sectors.

news.headline: Hang Seng +0.64%, China H2 fiscal support; aluminum prices set for +22% rise in 2026
news.what_happened: Hang Seng rose 0.64% to 23,768. China's economy gaining momentum in H2 2026 with fiscal support ramping up. Aluminum prices forecast to rise 22% in 2026 (World Bank), copper also gaining. China remains on 4.5-5% GDP target.
news.why_it_matters: Metals sector gets a dual boost: positive China sentiment and commodity price appreciation. HINDALCO (Novelis exposure), TATASTEEL, VEDL are direct beneficiaries.

sector_outlook.reason: Nasdaq +1.12%; FII buying expected; USD/INR stable at 95.38 supports USD earnings conversion
S&P +0.72%, US 10Y yield -0.13%, global risk-on; FII inflows expected into HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK
Hang Seng +0.64%; aluminum +22% 2026 forecast; China H2 fiscal boost for industrial demand
Crude +0.99% = short-term inventory gains for HPCL, BPCL, IOC
Crude +0.99%; jet fuel cost pressure on INDIGO, SPICEJET
Crude +0.99% = raw material cost headwind; ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT under pressure
Crude +0.99% = synthetic rubber, carbon black cost pressure; MRF, APOLLOTYRE cautious
India-UK FTA rules notified; effective July 15; preferential UK market access
RBI rate unchanged; no fresh domestic catalyst; mild positive from US yields easing
Crude up = mild packaging/input cost headwind; below-normal monsoon = rural demand concern

picks.catalyst: Nasdaq +1.12% on Thursday July 3; US tech sentiment at highs; FIIs expected to buy Indian IT Monday
picks.logic: TCS derives ~55% revenue from North America. Nasdaq +1.12% directly re-rates Indian IT valuations on Monday open. TCS dipped 1.72% on Friday (closing at 2057.6 vs Thursday 2093.5) — creating a dip entry opportunity. USD/INR at 95.38 means each dollar of revenue converts at a historically weaker rupee, boosting INR earnings. FIIs own ~15% of TCS and typically re-enter on strong US tech days. Risk/Reward from zone low: (2130-2047.5)/(2047.5-2020) = 3:1.

picks.catalyst: S&P 500 +0.72%, US 10Y yield -0.13%; global risk-on; FII inflows into India's most liquid private bank
picks.logic: ICICIBANK has ~46% FII shareholding and is the go-to instrument for FII re-entry into India on risk-on days. S&P 500 near all-time highs signals global risk appetite. US 10Y yield falling -0.13% is modestly positive for bank bond portfolio MTM gains. India's GDP at 6.9% for FY27 supports loan growth and NIM stability. Risk/Reward from zone low: (1477-1420)/(1420-1400) = 2.85:1.

picks.catalyst: Hang Seng +0.64%; aluminum prices projected +22% in 2026; China H2 fiscal stimulus ramping up
picks.logic: Hindalco is India's largest aluminum producer and owns Novelis, the world's largest aluminum recycler. Both domestic and Novelis revenues benefit from aluminum price appreciation. Hang Seng +0.64% signals improving China industrial sentiment — China is the largest aluminum consumer. 2026 aluminum price forecast of +22% (World Bank) provides multi-quarter earnings tailwind. Caution: already up 2.85% on Friday — enter only at zone low, reduce position size by 30%. Risk/Reward from zone low: (1005-975.5)/(975.5-962) = 2.2:1.

risks: Nikkei -1.19% on Samsung chip selloff — if Asia tech sentiment deteriorates further, may cap Indian IT upside
Crude at $69.23 (+0.99%) remains elevated despite OPEC+ supply increase — watch for airlines/paints selling pressure
GIFT Nifty +0.48% = moderate gap-up expected; stocks may open above watch zones — wait for price to settle before entering
FII net flow data for July 6 was not available — uncertain institutional positioning for Monday
Monsoon 2026 below normal per IMD — rural demand recovery risk; avoid aggressive rural FMCG/tractor positions
eod_summary: 0/3 picks hit target. TCS: +1.87% (target 3.5%), ICICIBANK: -0.86% (target 3.5%), HINDALCO: -1.11% (target 2.5%). Nifty50 closed at 24398.7 (-0.13%). Broad market weakness offset early global optimism.
